Title: SUMO-specific proteases SPF1 and SPF2 negatively regulate seed oil synthesis by mediating WRI1 deSUMOylation

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates seed oil content and seed size; loss of function increases oil accumulation, while overexpression reduces seed oil content.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. spf1-1 mutants showed larger seeds and increased seed oil content relative to WT. 2. SPF1 physically interacts with WRI1 and mediates WRI1 deSUMOylation. 3. SPF1-promoted deSUMOylation decreases WRI1 stability, thereby suppressing oil synthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis; overexpression; genetic interaction with wri1-3; RNA-seq; yeast two-hybrid; split luciferase complementation; BiFC; in vitro deSUMOylation assay; immunoblot; seed oil quantification by GC-FID; CHX degradation assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function assayed during seed development, especially 10 and 14 DAF; protein extracts from 10 DAF seeds used for deSUMOylation assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed developmental stage (6, 10, 14 DAF); cycloheximide treatment in degradation assays; NEM in deSUMOylation assays
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduced seed size and seed oil content
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Loss or attenuation of SPF1 could be used to increase seed oil content, especially combined with SPF2 manipulation, but potential effects on seed development and pleiotropic SUMO-homeostasis traits should be monitored.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts partially redundantly with SPF2 and genetically upstream of WRI1; homozygous spf1-1 wri1-3 could not be recovered, suggesting embryonic lethality in that background.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates seed oil content and seed size; spf2-1 increases SOC, while 35S:SPF2 reduces SOC.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. spf2-1 mutants exhibited increased seed area and seed oil content. 2. SPF2 physically interacts with WRI1 and lowers WRI1 stability. 3. Genetic analysis places SPF2 upstream of WRI1 in oil synthesis regulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis; overexpression; double/triple mutant analysis with wri1-3; RNA-seq; yeast two-hybrid; split luciferase complementation; BiFC; dual-LUC stability assay; immunoblot; CHX degradation assay; seed oil quantification by GC-FID
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function assayed during seed development, especially 10 and 14 DAF
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed developmental stage (6, 10, 14 DAF); cycloheximide treatment in degradation assays
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduced seed size and seed oil content
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Downregulation or editing of SPF2 may elevate seed oil content and could be combined with SPF1 manipulation, with caution for broader developmental roles.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts partially redundantly with SPF1; spf2-1 wri1-3 phenocopied wri1-3, supporting a WRI1-dependent effect on oil synthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carbon and energy storage in seeds
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2-domain transcription factor with intrinsically disordered regions
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; protein stability control via SUMOylation/deSUMOylation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Fatty acid biosynthesis and oil synthesis transcriptional program
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes seed oil accumulation and proper seed morphology; SUMOylation-stabilized WRI1 enhances oil synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRI1 acts downstream of SPF1 and SPF2 and is essential for their effects on seed oil synthesis. 2. WRI1 is SUMOylated in planta and in vitro, mainly at K257, with K266 making a secondary contribution. 3. SUMOylation stabilizes WRI1, whereas SPF1/SPF2-mediated deSUMOylation promotes WRI1 degradation. 4. Mutation of K257 impairs WRI1 stability and reduces rescue of the wri1-3 oil-deficient phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant and complementation analysis; overexpression; genetic interaction with spf1-1/spf2-1; in vivo and in vitro SUMOylation assays; site-directed mutagenesis (K257R/K266R); immunoprecipitation-immunoblot; confocal localization; CHX degradation assay; RNA expression analysis of downstream targets; seed oil quantification by GC-FID
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: SUMOylation by SUMO1/SIZ1-associated machinery; deSUMOylation by SPF1 and SPF2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates fatty acid synthesis genes including BCCP2, CAC2, PKP-β1, KASI, KASIII and other established WRI1 targets
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BCCP2; CAC2; PKP-β1; KASI; KASIII; FATA2; ACP family genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with SUMOylation/deSUMOylation machinery including SUMO1, SIZ1, SPF1, and SPF2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed in developing seeds/siliques, especially 10 and 14 DAF; high relevance at 10 DAF
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter; native WRI1 promoter (2,580 bp upstream)
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed developmental stage (10 and 14 DAF); cycloheximide treatment in degradation assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Very high WRI1 abundance may not further increase seed size, suggesting possible feedback inhibition, metabolic bottlenecks, or physiological upper limits
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator and essential positive controller of seed oil synthesis; loss of WRI1 overrides the elevated oil phenotype of spf mutants
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Stabilizing WRI1, especially preserving or enhancing K257 SUMOylation or reducing its deSUMOylation by SPF1/SPF2, is a promising strategy to increase seed oil content.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: K257 is the predominant functional SUMOylation site required for WRI1 stability and SOC regulation; K266 has a smaller contribution. Mutation of K257 does not alter nuclear localization but accelerates protein degradation.
